title: A natural-deduction inference rule: two blocks (premise and conclusion) separated by a bright horizontal inference line. In formal logic, the horizontal rule IS the certified deduction step. Precept's transitions work identically — the declared contract is the inference rule that derives the next state from the current state and event, with the same logical certainty.
conceptualBridge: The inference rule means Precept derives the next state by formal deduction because the horizontal line between premise and conclusion is the contract itself — cross it and the transition is certified; it cannot be crossed any other way.
decision: Shortlisted after iteration (avg 4.38): amber rule line made dominant and wide (v2); natural-deduction inference rule is a precise and specific conceptual bridge; the amber horizontal slab between two dark premise/conclusion blocks reads clearly at 32px; distinct from everything else in the set.
